title: Parodia calvescens
taxonomy: Parodia calvescens is classified within the family Cactaceae and the subfamily Cactoideae. It belongs to the genus Parodia, which is a group of cacti that are native to South America. The species is further divided into several subspecies, with some sources recognizing up to three distinct subspecies. The classification of Parodia calvescens is subject to ongoing research and revision. As a result, there may be some variation in how different sources categorize this species.

intro: Parodia calvescens, also known as the small ball cactus, is a small, globular cactus species native to South America, characterized by its short stature and vibrant pink or red flowers. The health benefits of Parodia calvescens include its potential to lower inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Parodia calvescens is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various skin issues and promote overall well-being.
